title: The Butter Shave

jokes.text: You know, I cannot stand this thing anymore. I know, I hate it too. I feel like an out-of-work porn star.
jokes.setup: Two characters expressing mutual disgust with something (presumably facial hair)
jokes.punchline: Unexpected, vivid comparison to unemployed adult film actor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant unexpected comparison that's both specific and absurd. Perfect economy of words with a vivid mental image.

jokes.text: What if we grew mutton chops? - No. - Buzz cuts? - Parachute pants? - Stop it, George. Stop it.
jokes.setup: George suggesting increasingly ridiculous makeover options
jokes.punchline: Jerry's exasperation at the escalating absurdity
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven escalation showing George's desperate brainstorming and Jerry's grounded response.

jokes.text: With crutches, it's a funny story. With a cane, it's a sad story.
jokes.setup: Discussion about mobility aids and social perception
jokes.punchline: Kramer's matter-of-fact assessment of injury narrative implications
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer logic - astute social observation delivered with characteristic confidence. Brilliant economy and insight.

jokes.text: He's like that fish that attaches himself to the shark. - And you're the shark? - Yeah. I'm the shark.
jokes.setup: Jerry comparing Bania to a remora fish
jokes.punchline: Jerry's confident self-identification as the shark
jokes.explanation: Good metaphor that reveals Jerry's ego and competitive nature. The confirmation adds character depth.

jokes.text: I don't know how a fish could eat laughs. Well, I'm glad I brought it up.
jokes.setup: Jerry's extended metaphor breaking down logically
jokes.punchline: Jerry's sarcastic acknowledgment of the metaphor's failure
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about the breakdown of Jerry's own analogy, delivered with perfect timing.

jokes.text: - So, what's this job? - Oh, it's beautiful. It's in sports. - Knicks? Rangers? - Playground equipment.
jokes.setup: George building up excitement about sports job with professional teams implied
jokes.punchline: Deflation to children's playground equipment
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection with perfect setup-payoff structure. Very George to oversell something mundane.

jokes.text: Well, I guess she's batted around and she's back at the top of the order.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Elaine dating Puddy again
jokes.punchline: Baseball metaphor for romantic cycling
jokes.explanation: Clever sports metaphor applied to dating patterns. Good wordplay with 'batted around.'

jokes.text: That guy's coming home in a body bag.
jokes.setup: Contemplating a month in Europe with Elaine
jokes.punchline: Darkly comic prediction of psychological destruction
jokes.explanation: Dark but effective hyperbole about Elaine's relationship toxicity. Great economy.

jokes.text: And tell me what time it is in New York, you are going home in a body bag.
jokes.setup: Elaine's mounting frustration with Puddy's tourist behavior
jokes.punchline: Specific threat triggered by time zone inquiry
jokes.explanation: Great callback that shows Jerry's prediction coming true in real time. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: Taking this lace out. It came undone, touched the floor of a men's room. That's the end of that.
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining why he's removing shoelace
jokes.punchline: Extreme germaphobia response to bathroom floor contact
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry behavior - his germaphobia taken to logical extreme with matter-of-fact delivery.

jokes.text: Well, I ran out of butter, so I had to borrow yours. Anything else, Mr. Nosy?
jokes.setup: Kramer obviously covered in butter, Jerry asking obvious question
jokes.punchline: Kramer's defensive response to reasonable inquiry
jokes.explanation: Classic Kramer deflection - treating the absurd as mundane and getting defensive about it.

jokes.text: Why are you buttering your face? I'm shaving with it. Oh, Moses, smell the roses.
jokes.setup: Jerry's incredulous question about obvious butter application
jokes.punchline: Kramer's casual revelation plus Jerry's exasperated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist concept delivered matter-of-factly. Jerry's reaction phrase is perfectly exasperated.

jokes.text: the natural emollients keep my skin silky smooth. - Feel my face. - No. - No, feel it. - I don't want to. Feel it.
jokes.setup: Kramer explaining butter's skincare benefits
jokes.punchline: Increasingly insistent face-feeling demands despite Jerry's clear refusal
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of discomfort. Kramer's obliviousness to Jerry's boundaries creates great cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Handicap? Oh, I'm not handicapped. I'm sorry. Differently... Advantaged.
jokes.setup: Boss trying to be politically correct about George's perceived disability
jokes.punchline: Escalating PC language confusion when George isn't actually disabled
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment highlighting PC language absurdity and George's deception starting to unravel.

jokes.text: Jerry, let's face it. I've always been handicapped. I'm just now getting the recognition for it.
jokes.setup: Jerry questioning George's fake disability scam
jokes.punchline: George's twisted logic about deserving disabled recognition
jokes.explanation: Brilliant George logic - turning victimhood into validation. Perfect character integration with dark humor. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: There was a guy that worked at the Yankees, no arms. He got more work done than I did... He drove a car with no arms? All right, I made up the part about the car, but the rest is true.
jokes.setup: George's elaborate story about armless coworker's success
jokes.punchline: Admission of embellishment when challenged on impossible detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect George - caught in obvious lie but only admits to the most unbelievable part. Great character comedy.

jokes.text: I love that bathroom. It's got that high toilet. I feel like a gargoyle perched on the ledge of a building.
jokes.setup: George describing his accessible bathroom
jokes.punchline: Vivid gargoyle comparison for elevated seating experience
jokes.explanation: Great visual metaphor that's both specific and absurd. The gargoyle image is unexpectedly perfect.

jokes.text: - Who? - The toothbrush in the toilet bowl.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking who Jenna is
jokes.punchline: Kramer's vivid metaphor for unattractive woman
jokes.explanation: Vivid, disgusting metaphor delivered with Kramer's characteristic bluntness. Great economy and memorable image.

jokes.text: After dating Jerry, you're a pleasure.
jokes.setup: Awkward introduction between Jerry's ex and Bania
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment that insults Jerry
jokes.explanation: Good backhanded compliment structure. Efficient put-down of Jerry while praising Bania.

jokes.text: I mean, the sex was good. Which I'm sure was fine for her... but I need more.
jokes.setup: Puddy oversharing to airplane passenger
jokes.punchline: Backhanded sexual self-compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Puddy - oblivious oversharing with unintentional arrogance. Good cringe comedy.

jokes.text: sometimes I ponder this silly gulf between us... and I say, why? Are we really so different? - For what is broth... - I'm not the one doing the cooking. Damn you, Seinfeld. You useless pustule.
jokes.setup: Newman's philosophical overture about their relationship
jokes.punchline: Instant return to vicious insults when Jerry deflects
jokes.explanation: Perfect Newman - fake philosophical depth immediately abandoned for petty hatred. 'Pustule' is a great insult choice.

jokes.text: You know, my last boyfriend, he had a real kroner comprehension problem. You know what I mean? A real cement head.
jokes.setup: Woman describing her ex's problems with Norwegian currency
jokes.punchline: Calling him stupid while mispronouncing 'cement head'
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay mixing currency confusion with insults. Accent adds to the character moment.

jokes.text: Oh, stick a fork in me, Jerry. I'm done.
jokes.setup: Kramer severely sunburned from butter application
jokes.punchline: Cooking metaphor for his burned condition
jokes.explanation: Perfect cooking metaphor for sunburn. Visual gag enhanced by verbal punchline. Good economy.

jokes.text: Technically, you're sautéed.
jokes.setup: Kramer saying he's 'fried'
jokes.punchline: Jerry's pedantic cooking correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry response - pedantic precision in absurd context. Great economy and character integration.

jokes.text: No, I read an article in Bon Appétit.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking if doctor recommended skin treatment
jokes.punchline: Kramer using cooking magazine for medical advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer logic - treating himself like food. The specific magazine choice makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: No, I fake limp on my right. This is a real limp because I sprained my ankle.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking about George's limp inconsistency
jokes.punchline: George now genuinely injured while faking disability
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - George becomes genuinely impaired while faking disability. Great character-driven situation comedy.

jokes.text: Oh, like what? Ovaltine? Why do dogs drink out of the toilet? Shopping carts with one bad wheel?
jokes.setup: Jerry listing Bania's supposedly good material
jokes.punchline: Catalog of hack observational comedy topics
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on hack comedy topics. Self-aware since Seinfeld itself does observational humor.

jokes.text: You like Bania's act. You're a closet Bania fan. - Maybe I am. - Oh, I'm gonna puke. Puke? That's a funny word. Puke.
jokes.setup: Jerry horrified that George likes Bania
jokes.punchline: George proving Jerry's point by finding 'puke' funny
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing George's lowbrow taste. The word repetition mimics hack comedy style.

jokes.text: Please, please. I don't want to get involved. I hope a giant mountain rises out of the ocean and we ram right into it... and end this whole thing.
jokes.setup: Magnus caught between arguing couple
jokes.punchline: Dramatic wish for death over continued exposure to their fighting
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully over-the-top response to couple's bickering. The specific imagery of a mountain rising is absurdly dramatic.

jokes.text: Yeah, now I'm simmering.
jokes.setup: Kramer in butter bath getting more treatment
jokes.punchline: Continued cooking metaphor for his condition
jokes.explanation: Extends the cooking metaphor consistently. Less impact than earlier versions but maintains the running gag.

jokes.text: But your cane's on the wrong side... That's just because we're standing on opposite sides... so the image was reversed. Like in a mirror. You see?
jokes.setup: Boss noticing George's cane inconsistency
jokes.punchline: George's ridiculous mirror explanation with physical demonstration
jokes.explanation: Peak George desperation creating absurd pseudo-logic. The physical demonstration makes it even more cringe-worthy.

jokes.text: You mugged Steven Hawking?
jokes.setup: George arriving with motorized wheelchair
jokes.punchline: Jerry's assumption about how he acquired it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor with perfect economy. The specific celebrity reference makes it more shocking and memorable.

jokes.text: It must comforting to know you'll be going to hell... at no more than three miles an hour.
jokes.setup: George's fraudulent disability scam
jokes.punchline: Combining moral judgment with technical wheelchair specifications
jokes.explanation: Exceptional combination of moral judgment and technical humor. Perfect structure and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Shut up or I'll snap you in half and stuff you in the overhead.
jokes.setup: Magnus making another comment during couple's fight
jokes.punchline: Elaine's airplane-specific threat
jokes.explanation: Good location-specific threat. Shows Elaine's mounting rage with creative airplane imagery.

jokes.text: His time has come... We're four months apart. - Nevertheless.
jokes.setup: George claiming Bania represents his generation
jokes.punchline: Maintaining generational divide despite minimal age gap
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic showing George's stubborn commitment to his position despite obvious flaws.

jokes.text: And what's the deal with cancer? I have cancer. Tough crowd.
jokes.setup: Jerry deliberately bombing with hack premise about cancer
jokes.punchline: Audience member's revelation making the joke painfully inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Exceptional dark comedy showing Jerry's plan backfiring spectacularly. Perfect timing and maximum cringe.

jokes.text: What you got there, the four volt?
jokes.setup: Newman confronting George about wheelchair collision
jokes.punchline: Mocking the wheelchair's low power
jokes.explanation: Good Newman mockery. The specific technical joke about voltage shows his vindictive attention to detail.

jokes.text: Turkey? A big, fat turkey.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking about Bania's performance
jokes.punchline: Kramer's emphatic confirmation with elaboration
jokes.explanation: Standard escalation. Works for character but not particularly original or memorable.

jokes.text: Look at me. I'm all covered in oregano and parmesan... and it's sticking because of the butter. Look at me.
jokes.setup: Kramer's butter application creating adhesion problem
jokes.punchline: Full transformation into walking Italian seasoning display
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual payoff to the butter storyline. The specific seasonings make it even more absurd.

jokes.text: Your legs.
jokes.setup: Boss discovering George can walk normally
jokes.punchline: Simple, devastating observation of George's deception revealed
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - two words that destroy George's entire scheme. The visual revelation is the real punchline.

jokes.text: I love stuff you don't have to think about. Give us a call. We wanna be in the Kenny Bania business.
jokes.setup: NBC praising Bania's material
jokes.punchline: Explicitly valuing mindless comedy over Jerry's intellectual approach
jokes.explanation: Good industry satire about preferring lowbrow content. Connects to George's earlier Bania appreciation.

jokes.text: Puke? That's a funny word. Can I use that?
jokes.setup: Jerry expressing disgust
jokes.punchline: Bania literally stealing Jerry's reaction for material
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Bania's parasitic relationship with Jerry extending to stealing his spontaneous reactions.

jokes.text: Hello. Oh, no. I'm sorry. Oh, no. No!
jokes.setup: Cab shortage forcing enemies to share transportation
jokes.punchline: Crescendo of horror as each person realizes their predicament
jokes.explanation: Good escalating horror sequence. Perfect ending that traps characters in their worst nightmare scenario.

tmdb_overview: Kramer uses butter for shaving--and more; George's cane is an advantage at his new job; Jerry feels used by his comedy nemesis, Bania; Elaine breaks up with her boyfriend before their flight back from Europe.
